title.en: Order got mixed up? ☕ How to complain politely in Korean
title.ko: 제가 주문한 게 아닌 것 같아요 (주문이 잘못 나왔을 때)

description.en: Don't just stay quiet! Learn how to use the "Softener" phrase "~인 것 같아요" to fix order mistakes politely and effectively.
description.ko: 식당이나 카페에서 주문한 것과 다른 음식이 나왔을 때! 상대방이 무안하지 않게 배려하면서도 정확하게 수정을 요청하는 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Food. Restaurant. Cafe. Complaint. Manners. Softener. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina, I was at a cafe yesterday and ordered an Iced Americano, but I got a Hot Latte instead.

content.speaker: John
content.text: I wanted to say "This is wrong," but that felt a bit too aggressive in Korean. What's the best way to handle this?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 한국에서는 상대방의 실수를 직접 지적하는 걸 조금 조심스러워해요.
content.translation: Right. In Korea, we are a bit careful about pointing out someone's mistake directly.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그래서 "틀렸어요" 라고 하기보다는 "인 것 같아요" 라는 표현을 정말 많이 써요.
content.translation: So instead of saying "It's wrong," we use the expression "~in geot gat-ayo" a lot.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "인 것 같아요"? Like saying "It seems like..." or "I think..."?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 정확해요! 내 생각을 조심스럽게 말하는 완충제 같은 역할이죠.
content.translation: Exactly! It acts like a 'buffer' to speak your thoughts carefully.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘은 상대방이 무안하지 않게 주문을 바로잡는 리액션을 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Today, we'll learn reactions to fix an order without making the other person feel embarrassed!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 제가 주문한 게 아닌 것 같아요.

content.speaker: John
content.text: By saying "It seems like it's not what I ordered," I'm giving them a way to fix it gracefully, right?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! "네가 틀렸어" 가 아니라 "내가 보기엔 좀 다른 것 같다" 라고 말하는 게 포인트예요.
content.translation: Right! The point is not "You're wrong," but "It looks a bit different to me."

insight.title: ~인 것 같아요
insight.usage_tip: This is the ultimate Korean "Softener." It makes any complaint or opinion sound much more polite and less confrontational.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 저기요, 이거 제가 주문한 게 아닌 것 같아요.
content.translation: Excuse me, I don't think this is what I ordered.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What if I want to be even more specific about the mistake?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그럴 땐 "주문이 바뀐 것 같은데 확인해 주시겠어요?" 라고 물어보세요.
content.translation: In that case, try asking, "주문이 바뀐 것 같은데 확인해 주시겠어요?".

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "확인해 달라" 고 부탁하면 사장님도 기분 좋게 다시 확인해 주실 거예요.
content.translation: If you ask them to "check," the boss will happily check it again for you.

insight.title: 확인해 주시겠어요?
insight.usage_tip: '확인(Check)' + '해 주시다(Honorific give)' + '겠어요?(Will/Would you)'. A very polite and standard way to request a correction.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. I'm at a busy restaurant. I ordered Spicy Pork, but the server brought me Soy Sauce Pork.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 저기요. 죄송한데 이것 좀 봐 주시겠어요?
content.translation: Excuse me, I'm sorry, but could you take a look at this?

insight.title: 죄송한데
insight.usage_tip: Using this before a complaint completely changes the vibe. It shows you acknowledge they are busy.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네! 무슨 일이세요? 음식에 문제 있나요?
content.translation: Yes! What's the matter? Is there a problem with the food?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 제가 매운 제육볶음을 시켰는데 주문이 잘못 나온 것 같아요.
content.translation: Ah, I ordered the spicy pork, but I think the order came out wrong.

insight.title: 잘못 나온 것 같아요
insight.usage_tip: Again, using 'gat-ayo' makes the interaction smooth.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 어머- 정말 죄송해요! 제가 주문을 착각했나 봐요.
content.translation: Oh my, I'm so sorry! I must have mixed up the order.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 아니에요. 다시 확인해 주시겠어요?
content.translation: No problem. Could you check it again?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네! 바로 다시 요리해서 가져다드릴게요. 정말 죄송합니다!
content.translation: Yes! I'll cook it again and bring it right away. I'm truly sorry!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Wow, it was so much easier than I thought! Using "인 것 같아요" really kept the atmosphere friendly.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 한국에서는 자신의 권리를 주장할 때도 상대를 배려하는 리액션이 정말 중요하답니다.
content.translation: Right. In Korea, even when claiming your rights, reactions that consider the other person are very important.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "order correction" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 제가 주문한 게 아닌 것 같아요!
content.translation: I don't think this is what I ordered!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 주문이 바뀐 것 같아요!
content.translation: I think the order was switched!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 확인 좀 해 주시겠어요?
content.translation: Could you please check this for me?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 제가 주문한 건 아이스예요!
content.translation: What I ordered was Iced!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 천천히 주셔도 돼요!
content.translation: You can take your time giving it to me!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 주문이 잘못 나왔을 때, 당황하지 말고 정중하게 말해 보세요.
content.translation: Everyone, when an order comes out wrong today, don't panic and speak politely.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "확인 좀 해 주시겠어요?"
content.translation: Could you please check this?

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 여기 주문 좀 받아 주시겠어요? (Could you take our order here?)
음식이 주문과 다르게 잘못 나왔어요. (The food came out differently from the order.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be mistaken / To mix up
vocabulary.example: 제가 날짜를 착각해서 어제 갔었네요. (I mixed up the date and went yesterday.)
예약이 잘 되었는지 다시 한번 확인했어요. (I checked once more if the reservation was made well.)
사람들 앞에서 실수를 해서 정말 무안했어요. (I was really embarrassed because I made a mistake in front of people.)
갈등을 줄이기 위해서는 대화에 완충제가 필요해요. (To reduce conflict, a buffer is needed in conversation.)
